questions.specification: What CAD simulation is specifically designed to calculate the flow of gases or liquids over a digital model?
Which of the following types of modelling involves using software to create and modify design representations?
Which of the following is a primary advantage of using Computer-Aided Design (CAD) software in the design process?

options.content: Increased time required for design iterations
Enhanced ability to create and modify detailed 3D models

questions.specification:
Professional landscape gardeners often use cordless electric hedge trimmers for extended periods. These tools must be designed to accommodate the physiological limits of the human body to ensure both efficiency and user well-being.

**Figure 1: A professional cordless hedge trimmer**

![A professional grade cordless hedge trimmer with an ergonomic wrap-around handle and battery pack at the rear.](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/61be7c9b-6490-449e-8742-50d4847e0c4f)

parts.content: Identify **two** factors that influence an individual's physical work capacity when operating heavy outdoor power tools.
parts.markscheme:
- Muscle strength (determined by age or gender). <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Cardiovascular fitness/endurance. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- General health/nutrition levels. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain why the **grip diameter** of the trimmer's handle is a critical physiological factor in the tool's design.
parts.markscheme:
- An appropriate grip diameter allows for a 'power grip' where the fingers and thumb overlap. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This maximises the surface area of contact to distribute the force/pressure across the palm. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Reducing localized muscle strain and preventing premature hand fatigue. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain **two** ways that environmental stressors can impact the physiological performance of the operator.
parts.markscheme:
- **Thermal stress/Ambient temperature:** <marks>1 mark</marks>
- High temperatures cause the body to divert blood to the skin for cooling (sweating), which increases heart rate. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This leads to faster exhaustion/dehydration and a decline in physical coordination. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Noise levels:** <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Constant exposure to high-decibel noise increases physiological stress levels (cortisol). <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This can lead to increased blood pressure and reduced concentration/mental fatigue. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain how the management of **Hand-Arm Vibration Syndrome (HAVS)** in the design of the tool can improve the safety, fatigue levels, and long-term health of the operator.
parts.markscheme:
**Safety:**
- Vibration-damping materials (like elastomers) are used in the handles. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This prevents the 'white finger' effect where blood flow is restricted. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Ensuring the user maintains full tactile sensitivity to keep a secure grip and avoid accidental drops. <marks>1 mark</marks>

**Fatigue:**
- Reducing high-frequency oscillations reduces the workload on the small muscles of the hand and forearm. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This prevents the muscles from having to constantly contract to stabilize the tool. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Allowing the gardener to operate the tool for longer durations without significant loss of motor control. <marks>1 mark</marks>

**Long-term Health:**
- Ergonomic design isolates the motor from the chassis using internal damping mounts. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This limits the cumulative exposure of joints and nerves to mechanical shock. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Preventing permanent debilitating conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome or permanent nerve damage. <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification:
A design team starts a project by creating a master 'skeleton' part that defines the overall volume and key connection points for a drone. They then use the geometry of this master part to generate the individual motor mounts and battery housing, ensuring that any changes to the master part automatically update the dependent components.

Which CAD modelling strategy is being applied?

Engineering firms utilize specialized simulation software to predict how mechanical parts will respond to physical loads and high temperatures. This involves mathematically dividing the 3D model into a mesh of thousands of small sections to identify potential points of structural failure.

Which of the following best identifies this CAD-based analysis technique?

An industrial designer is developing the curved exterior of a perfume bottle. They need to represent the complex organic shape and reflections on the outer 'skin' of the product but do **NOT** need to define its wall thickness or internal volume. Which CAD technique is most appropriate for this stage of the design?

options.markscheme: Solid modelling defines the internal volume and mass of an object, which is unnecessary when the designer only needs to focus on the aesthetic outer shell.
Wireframe modelling only represents the edges and vertices of an object; it cannot represent the 'skin' or surface reflections needed for aesthetic design.
Surface modelling represents the 3D 'skin' or exterior of an object without defining its internal properties, making it ideal for designing complex aesthetic forms and organic shapes.
2D vector drawing is restricted to two dimensions and cannot provide the three-dimensional representation required for organic shapes and reflections.

questions.specification: A dental student uses a virtual reality simulator to practice removing decay from a tooth. The system utilizes a specialized hand tool that provides physical resistance and force feedback, allowing the student to 'feel' the density of the virtual enamel. Which technology is enabling this tactile interaction?

The global packaging industry generates over 140 million tonnes of plastic waste annually, primarily derived from non-renewable petroleum. New international agreements, such as the UN Plastics Treaty, are compelling manufacturers to find sustainable alternatives to single-use plastics. In response to these pressures, designers are focusing on clean technologies utilizing algae-based polymers.

Algae-based bioplastics are carbon-neutral and biodegradable. Although biological plastics were first prototyped in the early 20th century, they remained largely ignored by the mass market until the recent focus on environmental resource management.

Clean technologies in this sector aim to reduce the carbon footprint of production while ensuring that the end-of-life stage does not contribute to oceanic pollution.

parts.content: Outline **one** way in which **public perception** of environmental issues encourages the industry to adopt bioplastic technologies.
parts.markscheme:
- <marks>1 mark</marks> Increasing consumer demand for eco-friendly packaging forces brands to change their materials to maintain market share
- <marks>1 mark</marks> Negative publicity regarding plastic pollution in oceans pressures companies to improve their corporate social responsibility (CSR) profiles

- <marks>1 mark</marks> Consumer awareness of the ecological impact of traditional plastics
- <marks>1 mark</marks> Leads to a shift in purchasing behavior that favors companies using clean technology

<note>Award <marks>1 mark</marks> for identifying a consumer-related driver and <marks>1 mark</marks> for a development up to <marks>2 marks max</marks></note>

parts.content: Suggest **two** factors that contributed to early bioplastic innovations being categorized as **shelved technologies** throughout the 20th century.
parts.markscheme:
### Reason #1
- Economic competition from the petrochemical industry <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Petroleum was abundant and extremely inexpensive during the mid-1900s <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Making it impossible for bio-based alternatives to compete on price without government subsidies <marks>1 mark</marks>

### Reason #2
- Material performance and technological maturity <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Early bioplastics often lacked the necessary barrier properties/durability required for food safety <marks>1 mark</marks>
- While traditional plastic manufacturing was already highly optimized and scaled for mass production <marks>1 mark</marks>

<note>Award <marks>1 mark</marks> for each of two reasons identifying why bioplastics were a shelved technology and <marks>1 mark</marks> for each subsequent development of that reason up to <marks>3 marks max</marks></note>

<note>Mark as <marks>3 marks</marks> + <marks>3 marks</marks></note>

parts.content: Explain the role of **international legislation**, **incremental innovation** and **end-of-pipe technologies** in minimizing the ecological footprint of the packaging sector.
parts.markscheme:
### International legislation
- Agreements/treaties (such as the Paris Agreement or plastic bans) set mandatory targets for carbon reduction <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This creates a legal framework that penalizes companies for non-compliance <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Effectively forcing the industry to invest in cleaner production methods to avoid fines <marks>1 mark</marks>

### Incremental innovation
- Focuses on making small, continuous improvements to existing products/processes <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Examples include "lightweighting" (reducing the thickness of plastic bottles) to use fewer resources <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Which provides immediate, cost-effective reductions in waste without requiring a total redesign of the manufacturing line <marks>1 mark</marks>

### End-of-pipe technologies
- These are methods used to treat or remove pollutants at the final stage of the production process <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Such as industrial composting facilities or wastewater filtration systems at the factory <marks>1 mark</marks>
- While they reduce the immediate environmental impact, they are often less effective than clean technologies because they do not address the root cause of the waste <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification: A bio-mechanical engineer is using CAD software to estimate the displacement and centre of gravity for a new prosthetic limb design. In order for the software to calculate these physical mass properties accurately, the digital representation must be a...
